documents.title: Accessory
documents.text: KonteX accessories are designed to extend the functionality, flexibility, and usability of the XDAQ™ acquisition system and X-Headstage™ platforms.

documents.title: Accessory
documents.text: This ecosystem of modular components enables seamless integration between probes, headstages, and external systems, supporting a wide range of experimental configurations in neuroscience research.

documents.title: Accessory
documents.text: From signal interfacing and system integration to mechanical support and cable management, KonteX accessories provide reliable and scalable solutions for both standard and customized applications.

documents.title: Tester-Stim-X3
documents.text: The X3 Headstage Stimulation Test Module is designed to verify the stimulus waveform generated by X3SR headstages. By connecting the module to a dummy 1 kΩ resistive load, users can safely observe and validate stimulation output characteristics without connecting to biological tissue.

documents.title: Tester-Stim-X3, Test Operation
documents.text: Due to the keyed connector pin orientation, the X3 Headstage supports left- and right-side connections. Users should alternate the connection orientation to verify stimulation waveforms on each side, ensuring complete testing while preventing incorrect insertion or electrical misalignment.

documents.title: Tester-Stim-X3, Compatibility
documents.text: Compatible with X3SR headstages

documents.title: XHS-Tool
documents.text: The X-Headstage™ Removal Tool is a custom-designed flat-head tool with tips specifically shaped to interface with both X3 and X6 headstages. It enables safe and controlled removal of headstages from connected accessories or adapters.

documents.title: XHS-Tool, Operation
documents.text: Use the included Phillips screwdriver to remove the screws securing the X-Headstage.

documents.title: XHS-Tool, Operation
documents.text: Insert the flat-head tip into the designated removal slot on the headstage interface.

documents.title: XHS-Tool, Operation
documents.text: Apply gentle, even force to disengage the connector.

documents.title: XHS-Tool, Operation
documents.text: Avoid twisting or prying at an angle to prevent mechanical stress on the connector or PCB.

documents.title: XHS-Tool, Operation
documents.text: • Use only the X-Headstage™ Removal Tool to remove headstages; improper tools may damage the connector or housing.

documents.title: XHS-Tool, Operation
documents.text: • Do not apply excessive force during removal.

documents.title: XHS-Tool, Operation
documents.text: • Ensure the system is powered off before removing the headstage.

documents.title: XHS-Tool, Intended Use
documents.text: This tool is intended to assist users in detaching X3 or X6 headstages during system reconfiguration, maintenance, or component replacement.

documents.title: XHS-Tool, Compatibility
documents.text: Compatible with X3 headstages

documents.title: XHS-Tool, Compatibility
documents.text: Compatible with X6 headstages

documents.title: Fine-Cable Organizer Kit
documents.text: The Fine-Cable Organizer Kit is designed for use with the Commutator Pro to manage fine cables with lengths of 1 m, 2.5 m, or 5 m.

documents.title: Fine-Cable Organizer Kit
documents.text: This accessory allows excess cable length to be neatly stored without wrapping cables around the Commutator’s rotating arm. It helps prevent tangling when multiple fine cables are used simultaneously, improving reliability and ease of setup during experiments.

documents.title: Fine-Cable Organizer Kit, Compatibility
documents.text: Fine cables with lengths of 1 m / 2.5 m / 5 m.

documents.title: Fine-Cable Organizer Kit, Compatibility
documents.text: Designed specifically for use with Commutator Pro.

documents.title: Fine-Cable Organizer Kit, Operation
documents.text: 1. Determine Cable Length

documents.title: Fine-Cable Organizer Kit, Operation
documents.text: Determine the required cable length based on the experimental setup to allow unrestricted movement.

documents.title: Fine-Cable Organizer Kit, Operation
documents.text: 2. Manage Excess Cable

documents.title: Fine-Cable Organizer Kit, Operation
documents.text: Place any excess cable neatly into the openings of the Fine-Cable Organizer, ensuring smooth routing without sharp bends or twisting.

documents.title: Fine-Cable Organizer Kit, Operation
documents.text: 3. Install the Organizer

documents.title: Fine-Cable Organizer Kit, Operation
documents.text: Align the Fine-Cable Organizer with the cable-retention slot on the Commutator Pro rotating arm and press firmly until fully seated.

documents.title: Fine-Cable Organizer Kit, Operation
documents.text: 4. Connect to the Commutator

documents.title: Fine-Cable Organizer Kit, Operation
documents.text: Insert the cable connector into the corresponding port on the Commutator Pro.

documents.title: Fine-Cable Organizer Kit, Operation
documents.text: 5. Secure the Cable

documents.title: Fine-Cable Organizer Kit, Operation
documents.text: Route and secure the opposite end of the cable using the Commutator Pro cable-clamping mechanism, following standard installation guidelines.

documents.title: Fine-Cable Organizer Kit, Operation
documents.text: IMPORTANT:

documents.title: Fine-Cable Organizer Kit, Operation
documents.text: Ensure the Fine-Cable Organizer Kit is securely mounted to the Commutator Pro with no looseness during rotation, and verify that all cables are firmly clamped and resistant to accidental pullout.

documents.title: Cable-IMU
documents.text: The Commutator IMU Cable is a dedicated communication cable designed to interface an external Inertial Measurement Unit (IMU) sensor with the KonteX Commutator system. The cable serves as the communication link that enables real-time transmission of motion and orientation data from the IMU sensor to the Commutator controller.

documents.title: Cable-IMU
documents.text: During freely moving experiments, animals may continuously rotate, causing tethered cables to twist and accumulate torque. The IMU sensor monitors the animal’s rotational movement using gyroscopic measurements and transmits this information through the IMU Cable to the Commutator. Based on the detected direction and magnitude of rotation, the Commutator automatically rotates in the corresponding direction, preventing cable twisting and maintaining a low-torque environment throughout the

documents.title: Cable-IMU
documents.text: experiment.

documents.title: Cable-IMU
documents.text: By actively compensating for rotational motion, the Commutator IMU system helps preserve natural animal behavior, reduces mechanical stress on implants and cables, and improves the reliability of long-duration recording sessions.

documents.title: Cable-IMU
documents.text: The IMU Cable is dedicated to motion-sensing communication and Commutator control functions. It does not carry neural recording signals, stimulation signals, video data, or other experimental data streams.

documents.title: Tester-Z-X3
documents.text: The X3 Headstage 64-Channel Impedance Test Module is designed to evaluate the impedance measurement functionality of X3 headstages. By connecting the module to dummy test loads with resistive and capacitive (R/C) values ranging from kΩ to MΩ, users can verify impedance measurement accuracy without connecting to electrodes or biological tissue.

documents.title: Tester-Z-X3, Test Operation
documents.text: Due to the keyed orientation of the connector pins, impedance testing must be performed in left and right orientations separately. Users should alternate the connection orientation of the X3 Headstage 64-Channel Impedance Test Module to verify impedance measurements on both sides, ensuring complete and accurate validation.

documents.text: Compatible with X3 64-channel headstages

documents.title: Tester-Z-X3, Compatibility
documents.text: Not compatible with non-64-channel or non-impedance-enabled X3 headstages

documents.title: EIB
documents.text: The KonteX Electrode Interface Board (EIB) series provides a reliable and modular interface between neural probes and KonteX X-Headstage™ systems.

documents.title: EIB
documents.text: Designed to ensure stable electrical connectivity, flexible probe integration, and efficient experimental configuration, the EIB platform supports a wide range of electrode formats and recording architectures used in modern neuroscience research.

documents.title: EIB
documents.text: Each EIB is engineered to maintain signal integrity while minimizing mechanical load on implanted hardware, making the series suitable for both acute and chronic experimental applications.
